Period:
Regulatory Transformation

Regulatory Transformation: navigating emerging risks, driving AI adoption and reducing regulatory burden

Regulators are facing a shifting operating environment as technology, markets, environmental pressures and societal expectations evolve faster than traditional regulatory frameworks. Fragmented data and limited intelligence continue to make risk prioritisation difficult.

There is a growing opportunity to use advanced analytics to better target regulatory effort. Many regulators are still working to integrate data across systems, uplift analytical capability and embed insights into everyday decision-making.

AI adoption is also becoming a core regulatory transformation issue. Regulators are seeking to balance opportunities for automation and improved risk identification with the need to manage governance, transparency, bias, legal risk, privacy and public confidence.

At the same time, governments are under pressure to reduce red tape, make faster decisions and manage fiscal constraints. This is driving interest in regulatory consolidation, shared-service models and more consistent approaches to regulatory delivery, while maintaining fairness and strong outcomes.

Regulation Hub Intelligence Brief

AI Brief 116 articles across 8 categories

Three regulator investment themes dominate the fortnight: ACCC's $67.7m enforcement uplift and AER's concurrent Victorian DNSP pricing trio plus Transgrid OT CPA signal a concentrated economic-regulation and OT-cyber spend; ASIC and AUSTRAC are simultaneously scaling cyber/AI supervision (26-092MR) and financial-integrity capability (new ML/PF updates, Financial Integrity Hub, VASP oversight) with live enforcement against Telstra Super, ABL Funds Management, Canva Group and Tabcorp; and NDIS eligibility reform plus ATEC stand-up and TEQSA's integrity push open material scheme-integrity and new-regulator build lanes. Clients squarely in the news this fortnight include Powercor, Jemena, United Energy, Transgrid, Telstra Super, Canva Group, Tabcorp, Veolia, Macquarie and ANU, alongside the Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission and AHPRA as buyers of governance and workforce uplift. KPMG should concentrate BD effort on the AER Victorian pricing-and-OT cyber pursuit cluster, an ASIC/AUSTRAC cyber-and-AML board-uplift offer to AFSLs and RSEs, and an NDIS eligibility-decisioning and data-analytics play tied to the Albanese overhaul. Secondary plays sit in ATEC/TEQSA governance and AI-integrity assurance for Go8 providers, and a CASA Part 138/BVLOS plus AMSA modern-slavery readiness package for transport clients. Treat the Inspector-General of Aged Care resignation and ACQSC Bulletin #4 as a 48-hour partner-call trigger into aged-care provider boards. Defence primes facing first SAMS Act charges and export-controls Federal Court action need an integrated personnel-security and end-use assurance conversation now.

Cross-category themes

Across portfolios, regulators are converging on a more aggressive, cross-cutting enforcement posture — ASIC, APRA, ACCC, AUSTRAC, TGA, TEQSA, CASA and AMSA are simultaneously escalating supervisory intensity, litigation and licence actions, with AI/cyber resilience, AML/CTF tranche-two extension, and personnel/sovereign security emerging as shared compliance fault lines amplified by the 2026–27 Federal Budget. For KPMG advisory, this translates into concentrated demand for board-level AI and cyber governance uplift, AFSL/AML/export-control program remediation, and transition readiness across major structural reforms (NDIS, ATEC, payments modernisation, network pricing and approvals pathways), with compliance cost bases shifting materially onto tranche-two and defence-adjacent entities not previously in scope.

Public Sector

AI Brief8 articles

The 2026-27 Budget locks in modest APS growth alongside an explicit productivity, regulation and tax-reform agenda, with The Mandarin flagging a push for faster approvals that will force agencies to retool assessment pipelines. ACCC's $67.7m four-year uplift is earmarked for additional consumer law enforcement capacity, while APRA's new AI governance letter and ASPI's analysis of $17.4bn in undecided Defence uplift signal concurrent capability and assurance demand across regulators. Advisory angle: package a regulator 'approvals acceleration + AI assurance' play that bundles target operating model, L&D and tech uplift for ACCC, APRA and Defence-adjacent agencies digesting Budget allocations.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Public Sector and Financial Services partners this fortnight to co-pitch ACCC on enforcement operating-model scale-up and APRA on AI governance assurance, and prime the Defence partner for the $17.4bn allocation decision with a costed capability-uplift proposition.

Energy, Mining & Property

AI Brief22 articles

AER is simultaneously running three Victorian DNSP pricing proposals (Powercor, Jemena, United Energy) alongside a Transgrid Operational Technology CPA with confidentiality claims and its inaugural wholesale gas market performance report — a concentrated regulatory investment cycle that pulls through revenue-model, OT cyber-assurance and market-conduct advisory. Federal moves to compress renewable approval timelines and NSW's uranium bill and energy-from-waste framework signal pent-up demand for environmental-approvals and social-licence work, while Victoria's EPA Supreme Court Landfill Levy win and Veolia payout sharpen enforcement risk for waste-exposed property and infrastructure clients. NSW's modern-methods-of-construction building laws and HIA's read of the 2026–27 Budget housing measures open a property/planning advisory lane. Position the energy regulatory economics bench plus OT cyber assurance for AER pursuits this fortnight.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: Brief the Energy & Natural Resources partner this week on a coordinated AER pursuit covering the three Victorian DNSP pricing proposals and Transgrid's OT confidentiality CPA, and line up the property/planning lead to engage NSW DPHI on the new MMC building laws and energy-from-waste framework before month-end.

Health, Ageing & Human Services

AI Brief14 articles

NDIS is the dominant pursuit signal this fortnight: the Albanese government's eligibility overhaul (forcing claimants to exhaust 'all appropriate' treatments) plus the Coalition's non-citizen access bar will reshape scheme integrity, eligibility decisioning and provider risk models — squarely in the assurance and data-analytics sweet spot. Aged care is simultaneously destabilised by the Inspector-General's resignation and a new Quality Bulletin from the ACQSC, opening governance-uplift and rights-based compliance work, while AHPRA's racism-elimination program and specialist-workforce sustainability agenda telegraph a multi-year L&D and workforce-design spend. TGA enforcement on listed medicines and the 28.5% medical cannabis sales contraction confirm regulators are tightening evidence and post-market surveillance — a clear pull-through for our regulatory science and compliance bench.

Implications for KPMGBrief the Health & Human Services and NDIA account partners before the Thursday parliamentary introduction to position KPMG for eligibility-decisioning, scheme-integrity analytics and provider-readiness work; in parallel, sequence an aged care governance pitch to DoHAC and ACQSC capitalising on the Inspector-General vacancy and Bulletin #4-2026.

Financial Services

AI Brief17 articles

ASIC is signalling material investment in cyber/AI supervisory capability (26-092MR open letter to AFS licensees and market participants) alongside an escalating enforcement posture — Telstra Super IDR failures (26-091MR), the ABL Funds Management AFSL cancellation (26-086MR), the Giumelli permanent ban, and a $792k Canva Group late-lodgement penalty all create immediate remediation and assurance pursuit leads. Treasury's payments modernisation reforms and the Government's review of the YFYS super performance test open a parallel regulatory-change advisory lane, with the 2026 super switching reforms and AFCA's SMSF-establishment standards driving licensee process redesign. Frontier-AI cyber warnings to the banks (echoed in ASIC's InFocus May 2026) position KPMG to lead board-level cyber uplift diagnostics across AFSLs, RSEs and major banks this fortnight.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: Brief the FS Regulatory and Cyber partners this week to co-develop a board-ready 'AI-era cyber uplift' diagnostic targeted at top-20 AFSLs and major banks, and stand up a Treasury payments-modernisation readiness offer ahead of the next licensee working group. In parallel, position the assurance and actuarial bench against the YFYS performance-test review and the Telstra Super/ABL enforcement wake to win IDR, trustee-governance and AFSL remediation mandates.

National Security & Justice

AI Brief15 articles

AUSTRAC is materially scaling supervisory capability — fresh 2026 money-laundering and proliferation-financing updates, a stood-up Financial Integrity Hub, and intensified virtual-asset registration/oversight following the reform commencement — all of which will pull through demand for AML/CTF program uplifts, model validation and Part A/B refreshes across reporting entities. The Tabcorp investigation (with a flagged $600m+ exposure) plus ASIC's finfluencer crackdown create immediate remediation and assurance pursuit leads, while ABF/AFP Operation Kurrajong and the NT's $5.9m illicit tobacco/vape taskforce signal sustained Commonwealth-state funding into border, excise and organised-crime disruption. Advisory angle: package an AUSTRAC-aligned 'VASP readiness + financial integrity' offer combining reg-tech tooling, transaction-monitoring tuning and board-level AML governance reviews, with a parallel illicit-economy assurance play for Treasury, Home Affairs and NT Government.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Financial Crime and Gambling sector partners this fortnight on a Tabcorp-style remediation play and stand up a VASP supervision readiness offer ahead of AUSTRAC's next registration wave. Concurrently, position the National Security & Justice bench with Home Affairs, ABF and NT Treasury on illicit tobacco/vape program design and assurance, leveraging Operation Kurrajong momentum.

Education

AI Brief17 articles

TEQSA is visibly stepping up its capability footprint — releasing role-specific academic-integrity guides, a contract-cheating investigators' manual, and accepting a voluntary undertaking from the Australian Early Education Institute — while ATEC's enabling legislation has cleared the House and the Commission has issued its first Letter of Expectations and advice notes, signalling a new regulator stand-up with attendant operating-model, assurance and tech build needs. In parallel, adverse findings against former ANU Council members and AFR commentary on TEQSA's ANU intervention are creating live governance-uplift demand across the Go8. The 2026–27 DEWR Portfolio Budget Statements lock in funding envelopes that PEPI should map against ATEC stand-up, integrity tech and provider remediation workstreams. Advisory angle: lead with a combined governance review + AI-enabled academic-integrity assurance offer to ATEC, TEQSA and at-risk providers.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Education and Public Sector partners this fortnight on a coordinated ATEC stand-up play (target operating model, risk framework, data/tech build) and ready a Go8 governance-review proposition off the ANU adverse findings — both should be shopped before the next ATEC advice cycle.

Transport

AI Brief19 articles

CASA is visibly investing in capability uplift across uncrewed systems (AusSORA BVLOS approvals, R&D consultation), examiner modernisation (PEGs in CASA exams) and a Part 138 surveillance program, while ATSB's portfolio budget statements and aerial firefighting safety review signal fresh assurance scope. Maritime enforcement is hardening — AMSA's Marine Orders 12/32 amendments on lifting appliances plus the Liberian-flag bulker port ban over crew exploitation create immediate compliance-uplift pursuit leads with port operators and charterers. NTC's Bulk Tanker Day 2026 freight-productivity agenda and the ACT fuel-supply disruption bill round out a fortnight where regulator tech, surveillance and resilience spend is converging. Advisory angle: package a CASA Part 138 / BVLOS operator-readiness offer and an AMSA modern-slavery + lifting-gear assurance review for shipping clients.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Transport & Infrastructure partner this fortnight to approach AMSA-regulated shipping and port clients on crew-welfare and lifting-appliance compliance reviews, and pitch CASA Part 138 / BVLOS operators on a surveillance-readiness and SMS uplift engagement before the April regulatory wrap-up cycle closes.

Defence

AI Brief4 articles

Defence-adjacent regulators are visibly tightening personnel and export controls: WorkSec has laid the first charges under the SAMS Act, putting personnel security governance squarely in scope for cleared primes and their supply chains, while Federal Court action alleging unlawful Australian arms exports to Israel signals heightened scrutiny of Defence Export Controls and end-use assurance. In parallel, Macquarie's $200m sovereign cloud and cyber build-out — anchored on Commonwealth demand — confirms sustained Defence and national-security investment in IRAP-aligned hosting and uplift. The convergence creates a clear pursuit lane: integrated personnel-security, export-controls and sovereign-cloud assurance offerings for Defence primes and Tier-2 suppliers preparing for SAMS Act enforcement and tightened export licensing.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Defence and National Security partners this fortnight on a packaged SAMS Act readiness and export-controls assurance offer targeting cleared primes and Tier-2 suppliers, and align the sovereign cloud / IRAP advisory bench to Macquarie-adjacent Commonwealth opportunities before the next capability gate.

Commonwealth

AI Brief14 articles

Federal Budget 2026-27 dominates the regulatory landscape, with material resourcing changes across Commonwealth agencies, a $13bn productivity package, contested housing tax reforms (negative gearing/CGT), and upward revisions to migration intake — all carrying downstream compliance and advisory implications. ASIC under Joe Longo continues its enforcement posture shift, with the chair publicly drawing accountability lessons from insurance-sector trust failures, signalling sustained scrutiny of financial services conduct. New sanctions on 230+ Iranian individuals/entities expand sanctions-compliance obligations for Australian businesses, while $600m+ in security funding (including $124m to ECAJ) reflects heightened protective-security policy settings. Advisory opportunities cluster around budget-driven regulatory change mapping, ASIC enforcement readiness, sanctions screening uplift, and housing tax reform impact assessments.

New South Wales

AI Brief28 articles

NSW is intensifying state-level enforcement and oversight: ICAC has opened public 'Pink Ops' hearings into Parramatta Council, the Auditor-General has condemned Health's $1.3bn VMO payment controls, SafeWork NSW is investigating Bluesfest, and a joint NSW-Vic early learning compliance blitz signals cross-border regulator coordination. Major legislative and regulatory changes are landing — new building laws covering approvals and modern methods of construction, fresh landlord penalties for premises selling illicit tobacco and vapes, and continued 'good character' reforms — creating immediate compliance obligations for property, construction, retail and ECEC clients. Public sector cost and governance pressures are mounting via the auto-triggered $1,000 CPI bonus, a $500m core policing system rebuild, $40m firearms registry investment ahead of Bondi/COPS audit scrutiny, and Transport NSW redundancy/mobility disputes. Advisory opportunities span construction approvals readiness, ECEC and workplace safety compliance, AML/illicit tobacco landlord risk, public sector workforce and IT assurance, and integrity/probity uplift.

Victoria

AI Brief25 articles

Victoria is rolling out significant new regulatory architecture, including the Gellung Warl Treaty body, a Commissioner for Better Regulation, a Commissioner for Economic Growth, and a newly operational Social Services Regulator and child safety regulator (the latter already facing governance scrutiny). Material legislative and rule changes are landing across building safety, OHS reform, retirement villages regulations, and EV charging infrastructure — each creating near-term compliance uplift work for advised clients. Enforcement posture is sharpening, evidenced by a heavy vehicle compliance blitz and a fresh education watchdog probe into school conduct allegations. The 2026 State Budget frames fiscal constraint alongside community safety and child outcomes spending, signalling continued regulator funding and review activity that KPMG advisory should track for clients in construction, transport, social services, aged/retirement living, and energy.

Queensland

AI Brief28 articles

Queensland's Commission of Inquiry into CFMEU influence over the former Palaszczuk government is producing significant evidence of regulatory capture at Workplace Health and Safety Queensland, including allegations of inspector 'hit lists' and rushed repeal of building laws — creating immediate advisory demand around governance, workplace safety compliance and construction-sector probity. The Crisafulli government is simultaneously fast-tracking major coal and gold project approvals, advancing further environmental reform, and has launched a state inquiry into the operation of federal (EPBC) environmental laws in Queensland, signalling a pro-development regulatory posture with implications for proponents and assurance providers. New e-mobility reforms (welcomed by the ICA) and exploration of the Taroom Trough add to a busy compliance agenda spanning resources, environment, road safety and insurance. KPMG advisory opportunities sit in CFMEU-inquiry remediation, EHS approvals strategy, project assurance for fast-tracked resource projects, and regulator capability uplift.

Western Australia

AI Brief31 articles

Western Australia is pushing through a substantial reform agenda with direct compliance implications: sweeping Residential Tenancies Act changes (ending no-grounds evictions, new bond handling deadlines) take effect alongside tightened tobacco and vaping retail laws and overdue child safety/working-with-children reforms. The WA Government has temporarily eased mining compliance settings in response to a fuel supply crisis, and the State Budget signals continued mining-sector support but limited red-tape relief. Federally, the ACCC has escalated the IAG/RAC Insurance acquisition to a Phase 2 review, materially affecting the WA general insurance market, while a potential WA university merger flags governance and structural change in the higher education sector. Advisory opportunities span landlord/property manager compliance uplift, tobacco/vape retail controls, child-safe organisation frameworks, mining regulatory variation management, and merger/competition response support.

South Australia

AI Brief18 articles

South Australia is advancing a significant fuel security law overhaul introducing emergency powers and on-the-spot fines, with an active enforcement blitz already penalising nine service stations for price compliance breaches — signalling escalating state regulator activity in retail fuel markets. A federal-state labour taskforce raided 18 SA vineyards, creating immediate compliance exposure for agribusiness employers under wage, sham contracting and labour-hire laws. The renewal of Serco's Adelaide Remand Centre contract and heightened childcare regulator intervention point to continued scrutiny of outsourced state service delivery and child safety governance. Advisory opportunities cluster around fuel sector emergency compliance readiness, horticulture/wine labour audits, and custodial/childcare contract risk frameworks.

Tasmania

AI Brief12 articles

Tasmania is advancing a dense legislative reform agenda spanning mandatory child abuse notifications, the Building Amendment Bill 2026, government contracting rules, councillor numbers/allowances, and granny flat planning liberalisation — creating immediate compliance uplift work across construction, procurement and local government clients. Child safety oversight is a flashpoint: a scathing report on government child abuse failures and Tasmania Police's stance on mandatory reporting will drive remediation, governance and assurance demand, alongside the government's response to a transparency review. Consultation has opened on the Aboriginal Heritage Act and new commercial permit fees apply for influencers in national parks, while the greyhound racing ban vote has been deferred. Advisory opportunities concentrate in reportable conduct/child safety frameworks, building and planning compliance, procurement policy redesign, heritage due diligence and local government restructuring.

Australian Capital Territory

AI Brief22 articles

ACT Government is advancing reforms to strengthen consumer protections and transparency, including strata management licensing recommendations and a review of the Working with Vulnerable People (WWVP) scheme following Access Canberra's unlawful handling of an application. Access Canberra is under scrutiny for procedural failures and delayed decisions, signalling heightened risk in occupational licensing and regulatory decision-making. The ACT Auditor-General ranks among the most independent nationally, reinforcing strong performance audit exposure for territory agencies, while the CIT CEO faces allegations of misleading the skills regulator. Advisory opportunities exist in licensing scheme compliance, regulator decision-making frameworks, strata workforce licensing readiness, and vocational education governance.

Northern Territory

AI Brief34 articles

The NT Government has introduced sweeping child protection legislative reforms to Parliament and announced an independent review of the child protection system following the preventable death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, though Indigenous commissioners, legal groups and the Children's Commissioner warn of inadequate consultation and breaches of Aboriginal child placement principles. A $5.9m enforcement crackdown on illicit tobacco and vapes is underway, with significant Darwin seizures signalling intensified excise and consumer protection activity. WorkSafe NT has launched a probe into PALM scheme worker housing conditions, expanding workplace safety scrutiny into labour-hire and accommodation standards. Advisory opportunities are concentrated in child protection compliance frameworks, Aboriginal engagement protocols, tobacco/vape retail compliance, and PALM employer workforce assurance.

  • Govt launches $5.9m war on NT’s illegal ciggies and vapes - NT News NT News · 10 May 2026, 05:30 PM

Clients investing in

Tech & AI

AER, ASIC and NSW are simultaneously moving on Tech & AI uplift, and the spend is now committed rather than scoped. Transgrid's System Security Roadmap Operational Technology CPA is in front of the AER, signalling a hardening cycle across OT/cyber that flows through to every TNSP and DNSP regulatory submission this reset. ASIC's 26-092MR is a market-wide call for cyber uplift in the face of AI-accelerated threats — a direct mandate to Boards of regulated entities and a green light for assurance, AI risk and incident-readiness work. NSW Police are back in market for a core policing platform after dumping the AWS-anchored stack, with a fresh $500m envelope and a parallel $40m firearms registry build accelerated by Bondi and the looming COPS audit. Treasury's payments modernisation reforms add a federal layer, with major banks and PSPs needing remediation programs to hit ISO 20022, NPP mandates and the new licensing perimeter. KPMG's closest hooks are PEPI's regulatory submissions bench for Transgrid/AER, Cyber & AI Risk for the ASIC response, and Tech Advisory plus Major Projects Assurance for the NSW Police re-procurement.

Australian Energy RegulatorAustralian Securities and Investments CommissionDepartment of the Treasury

Implications for KPMG: Brief the Energy, FS and NSW Government partners this fortnight on a coordinated Tech & AI push — lock in Transgrid on the AER OT CPA response, take the ASIC 26-092MR cyber-uplift play to the top-20 APRA-regulated boards, and position KPMG on the NSW Police core-system reset before the $500m re-procurement gate closes.

Show clients (3)
  • Australian Energy Regulator commonwealth

    AER is actively scrutinising operational technology cyber investment in the energy sector, with Transgrid's System Security Roadmap OT contingent project application (A.10 Confidentiality Claims, May 2026) now under regulatory review. This signals AER appetite for deep technical assurance over OT/IT convergence, cyber uplift economics, and confidentiality frameworks — pull-through for KPMG cyber, regulatory economics, and CPA assurance offerings across TNSPs and DNSPs.

    Implications: Brief the Energy & Natural Resources partner this fortnight on positioning KPMG for OT cyber CPA assurance and confidentiality-claim advisory across the next wave of TNSP/DNSP regulatory submissions.

  • Australian Securities and Investments Commission commonwealth

    ASIC has issued 26-092MR calling for urgent cyber uplift as AI accelerates the threat landscape, signalling a regulator-led push for boards and licensees to harden AI-era cyber resilience, governance and incident response. This opens immediate pull-through for KPMG cyber, AI governance and operational resilience advisory across AFSL/ACL populations bracing for heightened ASIC supervisory scrutiny.

    Implications: Brief the ASIC and Financial Services partners this fortnight on packaging an AI-era cyber resilience and board-readiness offer aligned to 26-092MR expectations.

    • 26-092MR ASIC calls for urgent cyber uplift as AI accelerates cyber threats - ASIC 2026-05-08T11:42:21+10:00
  • Department of the Treasury commonwealth

    Treasury is driving payments modernisation reforms — flagged in Ashurst's readiness commentary — that compel regulated payment providers and infrastructure operators to overhaul licensing, risk and technology stacks ahead of the new payments licensing regime and ISO 20022 migration. The reform creates near-term pull-through for KPMG across regulatory change, payments target-operating-model design, AML/fraud uplift and core-systems remediation among ADIs, PSPs and fintech clients.

    Implications: Brief the Financial Services and Payments partners this fortnight on positioning a Treasury payments-modernisation readiness play — bundling licensing gap assessments, ISO 20022 migration assurance and AI-enabled fraud controls — to ADI, PSP and major-merchant accounts.

    • Treasury's payments modernisation reforms: what it really takes to be ready - Ashurst 2026-05-12T18:57:01+10:00

Capability reviews

Commonwealth regulators are being explicitly recapitalised to lift enforcement and operational capability, with the ACCC securing A$67.7m over four years to expand investigations and the newly stood-up Australian Tertiary Education Commission publishing its interim Statement of Strategic Priorities — both creating immediate target operating model and capability uplift demand. AHPRA is under direct ministerial pressure to 'immediately improve' investigation timeliness and quality while CEO Justin Untersteiner pushes a specialist workforce sustainability agenda, opening a clear regulatory operations remediation play. DEWR's 2026–27 PBS and the wider APS budget signal modest headcount growth tied to faster approvals, productivity and regulation — meaning capability reviews are the precondition for the next wave of resourcing decisions. State-side, NSW Transport's mobility blowout, the NT child protection inquiry co-led by Karen Webb, and the Victorian Liberals' A$22bn APS savings proposition are all live capability and workforce review triggers. KPMG's Powered Enterprise, People & Change and Regulatory Operations benches are closest to the ACCC, ATEC, AHPRA and DEWR scopes; our Defence workforce modelling is positioned for the A$17.4bn ASPI-flagged undecided uplift. The pattern is consistent: regulators are being funded on the condition they prove operational capability, and that proof point is the engagement.

Australian Competition and Consumer CommissionAustralian Health Practitioner Regulation AgencyDepartment of Employment and Workplace Relations

Implications for KPMG: Brief the Public Sector and Health partners this fortnight on a coordinated pursuit of ACCC, ATEC and AHPRA capability and target-operating-model reviews, anchored to the ACCC A$67.7m funding decision and the Minister's directive on AHPRA investigation performance, before Q1 FY27 procurement windows close.

    The ACCC has welcomed an additional A$67.7 million over four years from the Australian Government to expand enforcement and investigative capability, signalling a material uplift in case-handling capacity and specialist resourcing. This funding boost will accelerate ACCC scrutiny across consumer law, competition and digital markets, creating immediate pull-through for KPMG capability-build, regulatory operating-model and investigations advisory work.

    Implications: Brief the ACCC and Federal Government partners this fortnight on positioning KPMG for capability-review, enforcement-uplift and investigative-operating-model mandates tied to the A$67.7m funding tranche.

    Implications: Brief the Commonwealth Health partner this fortnight on positioning KPMG for an end-to-end AHPRA capability and operating-model review covering notifications throughput, investigation quality assurance, and specialist workforce planning.

The Budget cycle is the trigger point for capability review scoping, Gateway assurance and target operating model work that KPMG PEPI can pull through across the portfolio's delivery agencies.

    Implications: Brief the DEWR account partner this fortnight on positioning KPMG for 2026–27 capability review, TOM and program assurance scopes tied to the new PBS measures before procurement gates close.

Learning & development

Four federal regulators are simultaneously rebuilding workforce capability frameworks this fortnight, and the spend is shifting from policy to delivery. Ahpra is publicly committing to sustain the specialist health workforce, signalling investment in credentialing, supervision and CPD redesign across the National Scheme. TEQSA has released a refreshed academic integrity suite plus role-specific guides for misconduct investigation — a direct prompt for university audit, assurance and training mandates as the GenAI-cheating wave hits enrolments. CASA is embedding Performance Evidence Guides into its examinations regime, rewriting how pilots and engineers are assessed, and AdviserVoice is flagging mandatory CPD on the 2026 super switching reforms that every licensee will need to operationalise. The common thread is regulator-mandated capability uplift with hard compliance edges, which is exactly where KPMG People & Change, Learning Solutions and Regulatory Assurance plug in. Ahpra, TEQSA, CASA and the higher-education sector are the highest-propensity buyers, with super trustees a fast-follow on the back of the CPD reforms.

Tertiary Education Quality and Standards AgencyAustralian Health Practitioner Regulation AgencyAustralian Securities and Investments Commission

Implications for KPMG: Brief the Health, Education and Transport sector partners this fortnight on a joint Regulator Capability Uplift play targeting Ahpra, TEQSA and CASA before FY27 training budgets lock in July.

Show clients (3)
  • Tertiary Education Quality and Standards Agency commonwealth

    TEQSA has released new and updated academic integrity resources, including a role-specific guide to promoting integrity and managing and investigating academic misconduct across provider workforces. This signals a regulator-led uplift in capability expectations that flows directly into provider training, assurance and investigation operating models — prime pull-through for KPMG L&D, conduct-risk and integrity advisory.

    Implications: Brief the Higher Education partner this fortnight on packaging a TEQSA-aligned academic integrity uplift offer — role-based training, misconduct investigation playbooks and assurance mapping — for Go8, ATN and large private providers.

    Ahpra is elevating specialist workforce sustainability as a strategic priority, with CEO Justin Untersteiner publicly signalling investment in pipeline planning, training pathways and capability uplift to shore up Australia's regulated health professions. This opens a clear pull-through for KPMG workforce strategy, learning architecture and credentialing advisory as Ahpra moves from rhetoric to programme design.

    Implications: Brief the Commonwealth Health partner this fortnight on positioning KPMG's People & Change and learning-transformation offerings against Ahpra's specialist workforce sustainability agenda.

    • Ahpra CEO Justin Untersteiner - Sustaining our specialist workforce - Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency 2026-05-04T10:40:55+10:00
  • Australian Securities and Investments Commission commonwealth

    ASIC is sharpening adviser learning and development around the 2026 super switching reforms, with CPD content now circulating on the process and compliance implications advisers must master before the regime takes effect. This signals near-term demand for training, supervision frameworks and compliance uplift across licensees ASIC oversees — a direct pull-through for KPMG's regulatory training, conduct risk and advice-quality offerings.

    Implications: Brief the ASIC and wealth-sector partners this fortnight on packaging a 2026 super switching readiness offer spanning CPD content design, adviser supervision uplift and compliance assurance for AFSL holders.

    • CPD: 2026 super switching reforms – process and compliance implications - AdviserVoice 2026-05-01T08:38:31+10:00

Risk & assurance

Risk & assurance spend is concentrated in three live pockets this fortnight: the ACCC has secured A$67.7m of fresh enforcement funding and will staff up investigative, economic and digital-evidence capability; the AER is mid-cycle on Transgrid's System Security Roadmap (operational technology CPA), Powercor and Jemena 2026–27 pricing proposals, and has issued its inaugural wholesale gas market performance report; and SIRA has locked a two-year reform window for NSW injury health provider rules. Health and human-services regulators are the other heat source — Ahpra is operationalising its racism and discrimination framework, the Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission is pushing rights-based monitoring through Bulletin #4 and the choice/dignity guidance, and the TGA is running targeted listed-medicines compliance reviews that have already cut medical cannabis sales 28.5%. NDIS eligibility is being rewritten around an 'exhaust all appropriate treatment' test, which will force assurance, evidence and decision-quality uplift inside the NDIA. AFMA's new Statement of Expectation and EPA Victoria's skip-bin blitz round out a clear regulator preference for visible enforcement over policy consultation. KPMG's closest pull-through is regulatory operating-model design, OT/cyber assurance for AER network determinations, Ahpra/aged-care conduct and complaints uplift, and ACCC-style investigations capability build — all areas where our PEPI bench has current credentials.

Australian Transaction Reports and Analysis CentreAustralian Securities and Investments CommissionTertiary Education Quality and Standards Agency

Implications for KPMG: Brief the Energy and Health Regulators partners jointly this fortnight to pursue Transgrid OT assurance, AER pricing-determination support for Powercor and Jemena, and an Ahpra/ACQSC conduct-and-complaints uplift package before the ACCC's A$67.7m hiring round closes the market for senior investigators.

    AUSTRAC has stepped up supervision of the virtual asset sector as Tranche-2 reforms take effect, issuing fresh registration questions for VASPs alongside 2026 money laundering and proliferation financing updates, and has launched a formal investigation into Tabcorp's AML/CTF compliance that markets are pricing as a $600m+ exposure. The supervisory posture shift creates immediate pull-through for KPMG across AML/CTF program uplift, independent reviews, VASP registration readiness, and remediation assurance across gaming, banking and digital asset clients.

    Implications: Brief the Financial Crime and Gaming sector partners this fortnight on a coordinated AUSTRAC play covering Tabcorp-style enforcement remediation, VASP registration assurance, and Tranche-2 program uplift for reporting entities.

    ASIC is escalating its risk & assurance posture, issuing an open letter to AFS licensees and market participants, calling for urgent cyber uplift as AI accelerates threats (26-092MR), and pursuing Federal Court action against Telstra Super for IDR failures (26-091MR) alongside $792k in infringement notices against Canva Group for late financial reports (26-090MR). The convergence of cyber, IDR, financial reporting and licensee conduct signals a sustained enforcement pipeline that pulls through KPMG demand for controls uplift, IDR remediation, cyber-AI risk assessments and regulatory reporting assurance across AFSL holders and super trustees.

    Implications: Brief the ASIC and Wealth/Super sector partners this fortnight on a bundled risk & assurance play covering cyber-AI controls uplift, IDR remediation and AFSL financial reporting compliance, anchored to the open letter and 26-092/091/090MR enforcement signals.

Implications will populate once theme synthesis runs in the next pipeline.

Regulation, regulator-hosted & L&D events

Upcoming events relevant to PSLG Leaders — regulator briefings, AICD/IPAA capability sessions, and L&D opportunities. Auto-refreshed each pipeline run; manual entries in context/events.json are the source of truth.

Event type
Regulation event
Regulator-hosted
L&D

Week of 2026-05-22

  • 2026-05-22
    AICD Boards & Regulators Lunch — Sydney
    AICDL&Dpublic_sectorSydney

    Partner-grade conversation between board chairs and regulator leadership on regulator expectations of director duty-holders. Useful BD touchpoint for FS and infrastructure pipeline.

Week of 2026-05-27

  • 2026-05-27
    All Actuaries Summit 2026
    APRARegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    All Actuaries Summit 2026

Week of 2026-06-04

  • 2026-06-04
    VIC Cyber Regulator Industry Briefing
    Office of the Victorian Information CommissionerRegulator-hostedpublic_sectorMelbourne

    Victorian information-protection regulator briefing on cyber-incident notification expectations and supervisory direction for state-funded entities. Brief VIC public-sector partner.

Week of 2026-06-12

  • 2026-06-12
    APRA Financial Risk & Resilience Practitioners Roundtable — Q3
    APRARegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    APRA-led roundtable with FAR-impacted entities; signal on operational resilience supervisory priorities for FY27. Brief FS partners ahead of bank/insurer mid-year reviews.

Week of 2026-06-16

  • 2026-06-16
    2026 AFR Insurance Summit
    APRARegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    2026 AFR Insurance Summit

  • 2026-06-17
    ABA Banking Conference
    APRARegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    ABA Banking Conference

Week of 2026-06-24

  • 2026-06-24
    AFIA Risk Summit
    APRARegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    AFIA Risk Summit

  • 2026-06-26
    ACCC Compliance & Enforcement Forum
    ACCCRegulator-hostedpublic_sectorMelbourne

    ACCC-hosted compliance forum covering FY27 enforcement priorities (digital platforms, unfair trading, supermarkets). Pursue brief for retail, telco, and digital partners.

Week of 2026-07-09

  • 2026-07-09
    ASIC Enforcement Update Webinar — Q3 2026
    ASICRegulator-hostedfinancial_servicesOnline

    Quarterly enforcement priorities readout from ASIC Commissioners. Direct read into FS and listed-company supervisory tone for the next 6 months.

Week of 2026-07-23

  • 2026-07-23
    KPMG Regulatory Practice Masterclass — RT CoP
    KPMGL&Dpublic_sectorOnline

    Internal CoP masterclass on duty-holder experience and risk-based regulation, drawing on current PSLG engagements. Partner/Director attendance encouraged.

Week of 2026-08-13

  • 2026-08-13 – 2026-08-14
    IPAA National Regulatory Governance Forum 2026
    IPAARegulation eventpublic_sectorCanberra

    Two-day national forum on regulatory stewardship, RPPPF practice, and cross-jurisdictional regulator capability. Strong PSLG attendance expected; CoP-relevant.

Week of 2026-08-20

  • 2026-08-20
    AHRI Public Sector L&D Symposium
    AHRIL&Dpublic_sectorSydney

    AHRI symposium on public-sector workforce capability and regulator L&D investment. Useful read on capability-reviews market for NSW/Cwlth pipeline.

No upcoming events match these filters. Check back after the next refresh.

Australian Broadcasting Corporation
Showing 0 regulators

State-gov priorities

Cited strategic priorities across all 9 jurisdictions, refreshed bi-annually. Every source URL is .gov.au.

Housing supply is the most universal pressure point, with ACT (30,000 homes by 2030), NSW (TOD 170,000+), QLD (1 million by 2046), VIC (800,000 by 2034), SA, TAS (10,000 affordable by 2032) and NT all running large, target-led plans — creating consistent demand for KPMG planning-system advisory, social-housing delivery assurance and developer/community stakeholder consultation across multiple states simultaneously. Energy transition and sovereign industrial capability form a second shared spine, linking Commonwealth Future Made in Australia, SA's Hydrogen and Net Zero strategies, TAS's 200% renewable target, WA's Made in WA / Strategic Industries Fund and NSW's manufacturing reshoring agenda, opening BD angles in industry policy design, grant program assurance, supply-chain due diligence and critical-minerals investment cases. Public-sector reform and integrity threads run through APS Reform federally, ACT's Parliamentary Agreement, the Wellbeing Budget approach and QLD's DPC strategic refresh, where KPMG can offer capability uplift, evaluation frameworks and outcomes-based budgeting support. Health and social-services reform is converging on workforce and community-care models — Strengthening Medicare and Support at Home federally, NSW safe-staffing, VIC's mental health decade and TAS's Healthcare 2040 — creating program-assurance, workforce-planning and digital-enablement opportunities. Jurisdictions diverge sharply on law-and-order versus rights-based agendas: QLD's "adult crime, adult time" and NT's Crime Reduction Strategy emphasise enforcement, while VIC's Treaty process and NSW's Safer Pathway lean into self-determination and victim-centred reform, requiring tailored stakeholder-engagement and impact-evaluation propositions. Resource and extractive priorities (NT's mining/energy/defence pivot, QLD's 30-year QRIDP, WA's Defence West) sit in tension with the southern states' housing-and-services framing, which KPMG can bridge through cross-border economic modelling, First Nations consultation and regional infrastructure business cases. Finally, large multi-year infrastructure pipelines — ACT Light Rail 2B, Sydney Metro, Bruce Highway, SA/WA 20-year strategies — present sustained gate-review, cost-assurance and benefits-realisation work that aligns directly with KPMG's Major Projects Advisory offering.

Last refreshed 2026-04-22. Bi-annual schedule; source URLs verified to .gov.au.

Commonwealth

  • Future Made in Australia
    The Treasury / Department of Industry, Science and Resources 2024-2034 (10-year, $22.7B package)
    Program design, sector assessment frameworks, investment mandate design, industry consultation, economic modelling, community benefit evaluation
    Source (2024-05-14)
  • Strengthening Medicare
    Department of Health, Disability and Ageing 2023-2027 (reform rolling); Budget 2025-26 expansion from Nov 2025
    Primary care reform evaluation, Urgent Care Clinic rollout support, workforce modelling, digital health implementation, Medicare integrity assurance
    Source (2024-05-14)
  • 2024 National Defence Strategy and Integrated Investment Program
    Department of Defence 2024-2034 ($330B Integrated Investment Program to 2033-34)
    Capability program assurance, workforce strategy, sovereign industrial capability priorities, AUKUS implementation support, capability reviews
    Source (2024-04-17)
  • APS Reform Agenda
    Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet / Australian Public Service Commission 2022-ongoing (4 pillars, 59 initiatives; Public Service Amendment Act 2024 enacted)
    Integrity and capability reviews, workforce strategy, stewardship framework design, data & digital workforce planning, contractor-to-APS conversion support
    Source (2024-12-01)
  • Working for Women: A Strategy for Gender Equality
    Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet (Office for Women) 2024-2034 (10-year strategy)
    Gender impact analysis, workplace gender equality evaluation, Government Board appointment strategy support, pay-gap economic modelling, consultation design
    Source (2024-03-07)
  • Aged Care reform – transition to Support at Home
    Department of Health, Disability and Ageing Aged Care Act 2024 commenced 1 Nov 2025; CHSP transition to Support at Home by 1 Jul 2027
    Provider transition support, data and digital strategy implementation, workforce modelling, single assessment system evaluation, consumer journey design
    Source (2024-07-01)

New South Wales

  • Safe Staffing Levels in NSW public hospitals
    NSW Ministry of Health Phase one early 2024; full rollout 2,480 FTE nurses/midwives by June 2027
    Workforce modelling, implementation assurance, change management support, clinical governance advisory, rostering system review
    Source (2024-03-11)
  • Transport Oriented Development Program (TOD)
    Department of Planning, Housing and Infrastructure TOD planning controls commenced 13 May 2024; 15-year horizon; 170,000+ homes
    Precinct planning, inclusionary zoning advisory, infrastructure funding strategy, development feasibility, stakeholder consultation
    Source (2024-05-13)
  • Sydney Metro – Western Sydney Airport and Sydney Metro West
    Transport for NSW / Sydney Metro Western Sydney Airport line opens 2026; Metro West completion by 2032; network to 46 stations by 2030
    Program assurance, capability reviews, cost estimation reviews, land use integration, precinct economic analysis
    Source (2024-06-01)
  • Bringing back manufacturing to NSW (state-owned energy and rollingstock)
    Cabinet Office / Treasury NSW / Transport for NSW 2023-2027+ (current Parliament)
    Industrial strategy advisory, local content analysis, state-owned corporation establishment, procurement strategy, workforce development
    Source (2024-09-01)
  • NSW Budget 2025-26 priorities (essential services + diversified economy)
    NSW Treasury 2025-26 forward estimates to 2028-29
    Fiscal strategy advisory, expenditure review, program evaluation, revenue analysis, investment prioritisation
    Source (2025-06-24)
  • Safer Pathway – domestic and family violence response
    Department of Communities and Justice Statewide rollout ongoing; DFV reforms 2024-ongoing
    Service design, outcomes evaluation, risk assessment framework review, cross-agency data sharing advisory, program logic review
    Source (2024-01-01)

Victoria

  • Victoria's Housing Statement – The Decade Ahead 2024-2034
    Department of Transport and Planning 2024-2034 (800,000 homes / 80,000 per year target)
    Planning reform advisory, activity centre masterplans, affordable housing economics, surplus land unlocking, stakeholder consultation, LGA target negotiations
    Source (2023-09-20)
  • Plan for Victoria (long-term plan to 2051)
    Department of Transport and Planning Released Feb 2025; horizon to 2051 (2.24 million homes target)
    LGA housing target analysis, settlement pattern modelling, sustainability and climate policy integration, First Peoples self-determination in planning, infrastructure sequencing
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Mental Health and Wellbeing reform (Royal Commission implementation)
    Department of Health 2021-2031 (decade of reform); Statewide Service and Capital Plan 2024-2037
    Service design, capital planning, workforce strategy, outcomes evaluation, lived experience engagement design, Mental Health Levy economic analysis
    Source (2024-07-01)
  • Treaty for Victoria / First Peoples' self-determination
    First Peoples – State Relations Statewide Treaty Bill 2025 introduced; negotiations ongoing
    Closing the Gap accountability framework design, service commissioning transition, policy impact analysis, governance advisory for new representative body
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Breakthrough Victoria Fund – research and innovation commercialisation
    Department of Jobs, Skills, Industry and Regions 2020-2030 ($2B 10-year fund; $1.3B co-investment leveraged by 2024)
    Commercialisation strategy, innovation ecosystem review, investment thesis advisory, co-investment deal structuring, industry sector analysis
    Source (2024-06-30)

Queensland

  • Making Queensland Safer – adult crime, adult time youth justice reform
    Department of Youth Justice and Victim Support (renamed 1 Nov 2024) Making Queensland Safer Act 2024 assented 13 Dec 2024; Queensland Youth Justice Strategy 2024-2028
    Program logic review, early intervention evaluation, victim advocacy service design, circuit breaker sentencing assurance, regional reset program evaluation
    Source (2024-08-01)
  • Homes for Queenslanders housing plan
    Department of Housing and Public Works 2024-2046 (1 million new homes by 2046; 53,500 social homes)
    Housing target modelling, modular housing economics, social housing procurement, church/charity land strategy, planning reform advisory
    Source (2024-02-14)
  • A Fresh Start – cost-of-living and tax relief
    Queensland Treasury 100 Day Plan completed early 2025; ongoing measures 2024-2028
    Revenue impact modelling, cost-of-living measure evaluation, payroll tax analysis, first home buyer stamp duty modelling
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Queensland Resources Industry Development Plan (QRIDP)
    Department of Natural Resources and Mines, Manufacturing and Regional and Rural Development 2022-2052 (30-year vision; living document)
    Critical minerals strategy, decarbonisation roadmaps, regional community transition planning, ESG performance frameworks, royalty policy analysis
    Source (2024-10-01)
  • Bruce Highway Advisory Council and regional infrastructure
    Department of Transport and Main Roads Re-established 100 Day Plan period (late 2024); ongoing through current term
    Regional corridor prioritisation, cost-benefit analysis, safety-focused project business cases, stakeholder engagement with regional councils
    Source (2024-11-30)
  • Department of the Premier and Cabinet Strategic Plan 2024-2028
    Department of the Premier and Cabinet (Queensland) 2024-2028 (current through 30 April 2025 review; superseded by 2025-2029 plan)
    Central agency capability review, Cabinet support modernisation, policy quality uplift, intergovernmental strategy, corporate excellence frameworks
    Source (2024-06-28)

Western Australia

  • WA Government Priorities 2025-2029 (Jobs, Health, Housing, Community, Environment, Infrastructure and Services)
    Department of the Premier and Cabinet (WA) 2025-2029 (current parliamentary term)
    Cross-agency delivery coordination, outcomes-based budgeting advisory, capability reviews for new departments, program assurance
    Source (2025-03-01)
  • Made in WA Plan – $1B Strategic Industries Fund
    Department of Jobs, Tourism, Science and Innovation 2025-2035 ($500M additional investment; SIF total $1B over 10 years)
    Industrial policy advisory, biomedical precinct economics, battery manufacturing supply chain, EV bus and ferry procurement, screen industry strategy
    Source (2025-05-08)
  • Defence West: WA Defence and Defence Industry Strategy 2025
    Department of Jobs, Tourism, Science and Innovation / Defence West 2025-2040 (15-year strategic vision)
    AUKUS readiness, continuous naval shipbuilding industrial strategy, defence workforce planning, supplier diversification, sovereign capability analysis
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Diversify WA: 2024 Update (economic development framework)
    Department of Jobs, Tourism, Science and Innovation Launched 2019; updated 2024; nine external-facing sectors
    Sector prioritisation, investment attraction strategy, comparative advantage analysis, export market development, regulatory environment review
    Source (2024-09-01)
  • State Infrastructure Strategy (Infrastructure WA)
    Infrastructure WA 20-year strategy; IWA Annual Report and Government Response
    Infrastructure business case review, prioritisation methodology, capital program assurance, decarbonisation of infrastructure, asset recycling analysis
    Source (2024-01-01)

South Australia

  • State Prosperity Project
    Department of the Premier and Cabinet (SA) / Department for Energy and Mining Announced 2023; long-term (renewables, critical minerals, green manufacturing)
    Critical minerals strategy, green steel economics, Northern Water business case, green industrial precinct design, trade and investment attraction
    Source (2023-09-01)
  • South Australia's Net Zero Strategy 2024-2030
    Department for Environment and Water / Department for Energy and Mining 2024-2030 (100% net renewable electricity by 2027; net zero by 2050)
    Decarbonisation pathways, hydrogen industry business case, circular economy policy, climate risk disclosure, renewable energy grid modelling
    Source (2024-01-01)
  • Hydrogen Jobs Plan and South Australia's Hydrogen Action Plan
    Office of Hydrogen Power South Australia (OHPSA) $593M green hydrogen power facility; world-leading hydrogen facilities 2026; 20 actions across 5 themes
    Hydrogen offtake structuring, green hydrogen cost modelling, project finance advisory, regulatory pathway analysis under HRE Act
    Source (2024-01-01)
  • SA State Budget 2025-26: housing, health, cost of living, skills
    South Australian Department of Treasury and Finance 2025-26 forward estimates; $2.5B health over 5 years; $843.6M housing
    Fiscal strategy advisory, housing project economics (Playford Alive East, Seaton), mental health co-responder evaluation, skills investment evaluation
    Source (2025-06-05)
  • 20-Year State Infrastructure Strategy
    Infrastructure SA 20-year horizon; update cycles every 5 years
    Business case assurance, infrastructure prioritisation methodology, asset management strategy, capital program reviews
    Source (2024-01-01)

Tasmania

  • 2030 Strong Plan for Tasmania's Future
    Department of Premier and Cabinet (Tasmania) 2024-2030 (delivered via 2024-25 Budget and onwards)
    Whole-of-government program delivery assurance, cross-cutting priorities alignment, strategic investment review, outcome reporting frameworks
    Source (2024-09-12)
  • Long-Term Plan for Healthcare in Tasmania 2040 / Our Healthcare Future
    Department of Health (Tasmania) 2040 horizon; Our Healthcare Future released December 2022 with 6 action areas
    Primary/acute balance modelling, workforce strategy (especially rural/regional), integrated care pathways, digital health roadmap, demand modelling
    Source (2023-06-01)
  • Tasmanian Renewable Energy Target – 200% by 2040
    Department of State Growth / ReCFIT (Renewables, Climate and Future Industries Tasmania) 2020-2040 (200% renewable generation; Bell Bay green hydrogen hub by 2030)
    REZ business case support, Renewable Energy Approvals Pathway (REAP) process design, green hydrogen offtake analysis, transmission strategy, workforce planning
    Source (2024-11-01)
  • Tasmanian Housing Strategy – 10,000 affordable homes by 2032
    Department of State Growth / Homes Tasmania 2022-2032 ($550M over 4 years)
    Social housing project feasibility, community housing sector capacity, modular construction economics, land release strategy
    Source (2024-09-12)
  • Tasmanian Tourism Strategy – 1.6M interstate + 400K international visitors by 2030
    Department of State Growth / Tourism Tasmania 2024-2030 (target: $5.6B visitor spend)
    Visitor economy modelling, regional dispersal strategy, aviation access, destination marketing ROI, nature-based tourism economics
    Source (2024-12-01)

Australian Capital Territory

  • Parliamentary and Governing Agreement for the 10th ACT Legislative Assembly
    Chief Minister, Treasury and Economic Development Directorate (CMTEDD) 2024-2028 (10th Legislative Assembly term)
    Whole-of-government priority alignment, directorate capability reviews, outcome-based reporting framework design, reform program assurance
    Source (2024-10-20)
  • Housing for All – 30,000 new homes by 2030 and 5,000 new social/affordable/public homes
    Housing ACT / Chief Minister, Treasury and Economic Development Directorate 2024-2030 (30,000 homes; 5,000 social/affordable)
    Land release strategy, social housing procurement, Territory Plan implementation, affordable housing economics, construction pipeline modelling
    Source (2024-06-25)
  • 2024-25 Infrastructure Investment Program ($8B over 5 years)
    ACT Treasury / Infrastructure Canberra / Major Projects Canberra 2024-2029 ($1.4B in 2024-25; $8.1B over 5 years to 2028-29)
    Capital program assurance, business case reviews, PPP structuring advisory, precinct development, infrastructure decarbonisation
    Source (2024-06-25)
  • Light Rail Stage 2B (Commonwealth Park to Woden)
    Transport Canberra and City Services Directorate Draft EIS public comment July-Sept 2025; delivery subject to approvals
    Project assurance, heritage impact assessment, urban design advisory, National Triangle stakeholder engagement, wire-free technology economics
    Source (2024-07-07)
  • ACT Wellbeing Framework and 2024-25 Wellbeing Budget Statement
    Chief Minister, Treasury and Economic Development Directorate (CMTEDD) Framework operational since 2020; Budget-integrated from 2020-21 onwards
    Wellbeing-informed policy evaluation, outcome measurement, cost-benefit analysis incorporating wellbeing, priority population analysis, co-benefits assessment
    Source (2024-06-25)
  • ACT Climate Change Strategy 2026-2035 (under development) / Active Travel Plan 2024-30
    Environment, Planning and Sustainable Development Directorate New Climate Strategy 2026-2035 under development; Active Travel Plan 2024-2030; net zero emissions by 2045
    Emissions reduction pathways, transport decarbonisation, gas phase-out strategy, just transition planning, EV fleet procurement
    Source (2024-11-01)

Northern Territory

  • Rebuilding the Economy: Northern Territory Economic Strategy 2025
    Department of the Chief Minister and Cabinet (NT) / Office of the Territory Coordinator 2025-2028 (5 strategic priorities; 20 strategic initiatives for immediate implementation)
    Major project approvals process design, investment attraction, regional growth prioritisation, Territory workforce strategy, industry sector strategies
    Source (2025-03-01)
  • Reducing Crime: Northern Territory Crime Reduction Strategy 2025
    Department of the Attorney-General and Justice / NT Police 2024-2027 (3-year strategy; review after 3 years)
    Criminal justice system reform evaluation, early intervention program design, youth diversion evaluation, data-driven policing analysis, bail reform impact assessment
    Source (2024-12-01)
  • Restoring the Territory Lifestyle Strategy
    Department of the Chief Minister and Cabinet (NT) 2024-2028 (current parliamentary term)
    Parks/recreation asset management, community infrastructure investment, sporting facility modernisation, tariff subsidy economic analysis, cost of living measures
    Source (2024-12-01)
  • HomeGrown Territory and housing incentives package
    Department of Lands, Planning and the Environment / Treasury NT 2024-ongoing (current term)
    First home buyer grant economic analysis, construction capacity review, payroll tax relief modelling, land supply strategy
    Source (2025-01-01)
  • NT 2025-26 Budget priority sectors: energy, defence, agriculture, mining, tourism
    Northern Territory Treasury / Department of Trade, Business and Asian Relations 2025-26 forward estimates (4 years)
    Barossa gas project economics, Beetaloo Basin onshore gas analysis, cattle industry export modelling, defence precinct capability advisory, tourism recovery strategy
    Source (2025-05-13)
